title: Mounting a single file creates directory on container instaed
body:
### Issue Description

Note: I am running in docker compatibility mode.
Note: I have only tried running this via podman-compose, but I pulled down podman-compose checked the outputs of the Python script and it appears to be giving the correct values for this to work.

Mounting a single file to a container incorrectly mounts it as a directory. For example, attempting to pass the flag `-v "/etc/ssl/certs/root.pem:/etc/ssl/certs/root.pem:ro"` results in the /etc/ssl/certs/root.pem on the container being a directory. I am also passing `-v /var/run/docker.sock:/var/run/docker.sock:ro` which is working correctly.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Attempt to attach a single file as a volume to a container as readonly: `<my_file_path>:<container_file_path>:ro`
2. Run `docker exec -it <my_container> ls -l <container_file_path_directory>`
3. Observe the file being incorrectly marked as a directory


### Describe the results you received

Running `docker exec -it my_container ls -l /etc/ssl/certs` creates the output:
```sh
-rw-r--r--. 1 root root 236849 Jan 31 12:12 cacert.pem
drwxr-xr-x. 2 root root      6 Feb  3 19:08 root.pem
```

### Describe the results you expected

Describe the results you expectedRunning `docker exec -it my_container ls -l /etc/ssl/certs` creates the output:
```sh
-rw-r--r--. 1 root root 236849 Jan 31 12:12 cacert.pem
-rw-r--r--. 1 root root      6 Feb  3 19:08 root.pem
```

### Additional information

_No response_

comments.body:
Does `/etc/ssl/certs/root.pem` exists in your VM machine (check with `podman machine ssh`)? I would think not and in that case the docker compatible API  creates the source directory for the mount.

If you use normal podman it should error as the source does not exists

@Luap99 I tried again this morning checking against both my machine and the VM machine. The file is there on both machines. In fact, I tried three different paths:
```md
      - /etc/ssl/root.pem:/etc/ssl/host-root.pem:ro # exists on the host machine
      - /etc/ssl/ROOT.pem:/etc/ssl/vm-root.pem:ro # exists on the VM machine
      - /Users/myuser/Documents/certs/root.pem:/etc/ssl/certs/host-documnets-root.pem:ro # exists on the host machine 
```
I only see the `host-documents-root.pem` being mounted as a directory

EDIT:
I see now that my path was incorrect on the target side of the mount path 🤦🏻 
After making the correct changes, I now see the `vm-root.pem` showing up correctly as a single file

@apederson94 Just a hail Mary. I am also trying to mount a single file and it mounts as a directory. Giving an example:

```
some_web_ui:
     image: someImage:imageTag
     ports:
       - 3006:8080
     networks:
        - default
     environment:
        SOME_VARIABLES: theirValues
     volumes:
        - ./config/config.js:/tmp/config.js:ro # The intended file
```

Expecting to see a file at the target path, but within the `tmp` directory a `config.js` directory is created instead. Any quick ideas of what you used to escape this hell?
